plan_metadata.name: Feature Publish to Teams Build Package If Not Provision

steps.description: Click on the "Teams Agents and Apps" option within the dropdown menu under the "New Project" dialog in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the "Other Teams Capability" option within the dropdown menu titled "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu.
Click the "JavaScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Default folder" option (/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects) in the "Workspace Folder" dropdown within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in the Visual Studio Code application interface, with the Explorer panel displaying the project structure.
Type '>microsoft 365 agents: zip app package' into the Command Palette input field in Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the command "Microsoft 365 Agents: Zip App Package" from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Click on the "manifest.json" file selection option within the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog box in Visual Studio Code interface.
Click on the "dev" option within the dropdown menu labeled "Select an environment" in the Visual Studio Code interface.
@assertion "Missing environment variables 'BOT_ID'"" is visible

Click the "Clear Notification (Delete)" button within the error tooltip displayed at the bottom-right of the Visual Studio Code interface. The tooltip highlights a missing environment variable error from the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.
Press the F1 key to open the command palette in Visual Studio Code within the project directory "vscuse_app_yjfrx".
Type the text: 'microsoft 365 agents: validate application' in the VS Code command palette, located at the top center of the Visual Studio Code interface, activated by pressing `Ctrl+Shift+P`.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the command in the Visual Studio Code command palette after selecting "Microsoft 365 Agents: Validate Application."

Type text: 'validate app manifest schema' into the input field located in the "Select a validation method" dialog box within the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface, part of the Microsoft 365 Toolkit extension environment.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "Validate app manifest schema" option from the "Select a validation method" dropdown bar in the Visual Studio Code interface.

Click on the file named "manifest.json" located within the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog box in the Visual Studio Code interface, under the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects/vscuse_app_yjfrx/appPackage".
Type text: 'dev' into the "Select an environment" dropdown input field in the Visual Studio Code interface.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the "dev" environment from the dropdown in the VS Code application context.
@assertion "Missing environment variables 'BOT_ID'"" is visible
Click the "Get Help" button in the error notification pane of the Microsoft Visual Studio Code interface.
Use keyboard shortcut "ctrl+j" to toggle the visibility of the integrated terminal in Visual Studio Code while working in the "VSCUSE_APP_NKdbt" project.
Press the "F1" key to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension interface.
Type text into the "Command Palette" input field located at the top center of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface: 'microsoft 365 agents: zip app package'.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the command ">microsoft 365 agents: zip app package" in the command palette of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click on the **"manifest.json" file** displayed in the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Type text 'dev' into the environment selection dropdown located at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Press the "Enter" key to select the "dev" environment in the dropdown menu within the "Select an environment" modal in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
@assertion “App package successfully built” is visible

Press the F1 key to open the Command Palette in the Visual Studio Code application interface. The context is the "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit" workspace, visible with notifications displayed in the bottom-right corner.

Type text in the command palette search bar to locate and execute the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Validate Application" command within the Visual Studio Code interface related to Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.
Press the "Enter" key to execute the command "Microsoft 365 Agents: Validate Application" from the command palette in Visual Studio Code.
Type the text 'validate app manifest schema' into the dropdown input field labeled 'Select a validation method' located at the top-center of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Press the "Enter" key to select the "Validate app manifest schema" option from the dropdown menu in the "Select a validation method" field within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the file path **manifest.json** inside the quick access dropdown of the "Select Teams manifest.json File" dialog.
Type text: 'dev' in the environment selector dropdown, located at the top-center of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Press the "Enter" key to confirm the selection of the "dev" environment from the dropdown list in the "Select an environment" input field of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.

Click on the notification titled "Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit has completed checking your app package against validation rules. All passed. Check Output panel for details" within the "Notifications" panel of the Visual Studio Code interface to select or interact with it.
Press the "enter" key to select the "View provisioned resources" button within the "Notifications" panel of the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
@assertion “Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit has completed checking your app package against validation rules. All passed“ is visible
